summary: HarfBuzzSharp Font.SetPpem / Font.GetPpem wrappers are missing, causing HarfBuzz-shaped text advances to be measured without hinting, producing widths that differ from GDI measurements.

stepsToReproduce: Create HarfBuzzSharp.Font, call Shape() on a buffer with ASCII text
Sum buffer.GlyphPositions[].XAdvance * (fontSize / xScale) for text width
Compare with GDI MeasureString — values differ noticeably
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp.HarfBuzz v2.80.2, Visual Studio, ASP.NET Core, cross-platform (Windows/Linux/Mac target)

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Font.cs does not expose SetPpem/GetPpem as of current main; hb_font_set_ppem is only available as internal P/Invoke.

analysis.summary: The HarfBuzzSharp Font class exposes SetScale/GetScale but not SetPpem/GetPpem. Without setting ppem, HarfBuzz shapes glyphs without hinting, causing measured advances to differ from hinted renderers like GDI. The underlying C functions hb_font_set_ppem and hb_font_get_ppem are already bound as internal P/Invoke in HarfBuzzApi.generated.cs but have no public C# wrapper. A contributor confirmed this gap and stated the API needs to be exposed.
analysis.rationale: Classified as type/enhancement because the HarfBuzz C API is present and internally bound; the missing piece is the public C# wrapper on HarfBuzzSharp.Font. A contributor (Gillibald) explicitly acknowledged the gap. The area is area/HarfBuzzSharp because the fix is entirely within the HarfBuzzSharp binding layer.

keySignals.text: You actually need to use `hb_font_set_ppem`. I never exposed that API. We need to change that.
keySignals.interpretation: Confirmed missing API — maintainer acknowledged this needs to be fixed.

keySignals.text: hb_font_set_ppem is bound as internal static partial void hb_font_set_ppem in HarfBuzzApi.generated.cs
keySignals.interpretation: P/Invoke binding exists; only a public C# wrapper is missing.

keySignals.text: Font.cs exposes SetScale/GetScale but not SetPpem/GetPpem
keySignals.interpretation: Gap in public API surface — SetPpem needs to be added alongside existing scale methods.

codeInvestigation.finding: hb_font_set_ppem and hb_font_get_ppem are present as internal P/Invoke bindings (static partial and delegate variants), confirming the native API is available and bound.
Public Font class exposes GetScale (line 60) and SetScale (line 68) but has no SetPpem or GetPpem methods — the gap identified by the contributor.

workarounds: There is no public workaround to call hb_font_set_ppem today — the method is internal only.
Approximate workaround: set the font scale to the target pixel size (fontsize * face_upem / face_upem) before shaping; this aligns the units but does not apply true hinting.
nextQuestions: Should GetPpem / SetPpem be exposed as a property pair (like Scale) or separate methods?
Does exposing ppem fully resolve the measurement discrepancy with GDI, or is additional hinting configuration needed?

resolution.hypothesis: Adding public Font.SetPpem(uint xPpem, uint yPpem) and Font.GetPpem(out uint xPpem, out uint yPpem) wrappers around the already-bound hb_font_set_ppem / hb_font_get_ppem P/Invokes will allow callers to configure ppem before shaping, giving hinting-aware advance measurements.

proposals.title: Expose SetPpem/GetPpem on HarfBuzzSharp.Font
proposals.description: Add two public methods to Font.cs wrapping the internal hb_font_set_ppem and hb_font_get_ppem P/Invokes, following the same pattern as SetScale/GetScale.

resolution.recommendedReason: The P/Invoke binding already exists; only the thin public wrapper is missing. Small, low-risk change that directly resolves the issue.

actionability.reason: Root cause is confirmed (missing public wrapper for hb_font_set_ppem/hb_font_get_ppem), fix path is clear, and P/Invoke binding already exists in generated code.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and HarfBuzzSharp area labels
Confirm root cause and outline fix path with code example
